metadata.title: Why Silencing a Repair Gene Is Better for the Patient Than Expressing It
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=#1F6F5C = MGMT methylated / repair silenced / drug works / good patient outcome; CRIMSON=#BF3339 = MGMT expressed / damage repaired / drug neutralized / bad patient outcome
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no full DNA methylation mechanism (DNMT1, maintenance methylation), no histone code, no IDH mutation connection, no azacitidine/decitabine mechanism, no other epigenetic biomarkers. One mechanism only: MGMT promoter methylation silences repair, allowing temozolomide damage to accumulate and kill tumor cells.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Pragmatist register (voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md + HAI.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the HAI outro from AUTHOR.MD :: Humanitarians AI
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.name: Title
beats.narration_text: Two glioblastoma patients. Same surgery, same radiation, same chemotherapy. One responds. One does not. The difference is a single methyl group on a promoter.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Two glioblastoma patients. Same surgery, same radiation, same chemotherapy. One responds. One does not. The difference is a single methyl group on a promoter.

beats.name: TwoPatients
beats.narration_text: Glioblastoma kills most patients within 15 months. Temozolomide chemotherapy improves outcomes — but only for a subset. Predicting who benefits requires understanding one specific repair gene.

props.headline: Glioblastoma — the most aggressive brain tumor — kills most patients…

beats.scene_description: Two medical charts side by side, pinned to newsprint — same diagnosis header, same treatment protocol listed — patient A and patient B, editorial collage style, desaturated

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Glioblastoma kills most patients within 15 months. Temozolomide chemotherapy improves outcomes — but only for a subset. Predicting who benefits requires underst

beats.name: SameTreatment
beats.narration_text: Temozolomide attaches a methyl group to the O6 position of guanine in tumor DNA. That lesion jams replication and kills the cell — if the cell cannot remove it.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Temozolomide attaches a methyl group to the O6 position of guanine in tumor DNA. That lesion jams replication and kills the cell — if the cell cannot remove it.

beats.name: TheQuestion
beats.narration_text: A tumor that repairs DNA efficiently should survive treatment better. The patient whose tumor repairs DNA efficiently does worse. The repair gene is working against the treatment.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A tumor that repairs DNA efficiently should survive treatment better. The patient whose tumor repairs DNA efficiently does worse. The repair gene is working aga

beats.name: NaiveExpectation
beats.narration_text: Default assumption: more DNA repair is better for the patient. Treatment works by damaging tumor DNA; repair that reverses the damage protects the tumor, not the patient.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Default assumption: more DNA repair is better for the patient. Treatment works by damaging tumor DNA; repair that reverses the damage protects the tumor, not th

beats.name: WhyItFlips
beats.narration_text: MGMT reverses exactly the O6-methylguanine lesion temozolomide creates. When MGMT is active, it neutralizes the drug within minutes. The chemotherapy is wasted.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: MGMT reverses exactly the O6-methylguanine lesion temozolomide creates. When MGMT is active, it neutralizes the drug within minutes. The chemotherapy is wasted.

beats.name: DecisionTree
beats.narration_text: MGMT methylation status splits patients into two paths. Methylated promoter: no MGMT protein, O6-methylguanine lesions accumulate, tumor cells die. Unmethylated: MGMT expressed, damage repaired, drug wasted.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: MGMT methylation status splits patients into two paths. Methylated promoter: no MGMT protein, O6-methylguanine lesions accumulate, tumor cells die. Unmethylated

beats.name: SuicideMechanism
beats.narration_text: MGMT is a suicide enzyme. It transfers the methyl group from O6-guanine onto its own cysteine — one repair per molecule, then the protein destroys itself. It cannot be recycled.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: MGMT is a suicide enzyme. It transfers the methyl group from O6-guanine onto its own cysteine — one repair per molecule, then the protein destroys itself. It ca

beats.name: MethylSilences
beats.narration_text: In the methylated patient, cytosines in the MGMT promoter carry methyl marks. Transcription machinery cannot access the gene. No MGMT mRNA. No protein. Repair capacity is permanently off.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: In the methylated patient, cytosines in the MGMT promoter carry methyl marks. Transcription machinery cannot access the gene. No MGMT mRNA. No protein. Repair c

beats.name: Biomarker
beats.narration_text: MGMT methylation is a predictive biomarker — not prognostic. It does not predict survival regardless of treatment. It predicts benefit from temozolomide specifically. Use it to select patients, not to estimate prognosis.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: MGMT methylation is a predictive biomarker — not prognostic. It does not predict survival regardless of treatment. It predicts benefit from temozolomide specifi

beats.name: ExampleTrace
beats.narration_text: Patient A, methylated: O6-methylguanine forms, MGMT absent, lesion persists through replication, mismatch-repair triggers apoptosis — tumor cell dies. Patient B, unmethylated: lesion forms, MGMT transfers the methyl in roughly 2 minutes — cell survives, drug wasted. Illustrative trace.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Patient A, methylated: O6-methylguanine forms, MGMT absent, lesion persists through replication, mismatch-repair triggers apoptosis — tumor cell dies. Patient B

beats.name: Endcard
beats.narration_text: MGMT repair is the mechanism that determines whether temozolomide works. A methylated promoter silences MGMT, lets lesions accumulate, and kills the tumor. Unmethylated: MGMT active, drug neutralized. Test the promoter before treating.

beats.narration_text: This is part of the Cancer Biology series from Humanitarians AI.
